steps.title: Gather the source material
steps.detail: Start with the real inputs for turning one long video into short vertical clips: notes, files, transcript text, product facts, or examples. The output will only be useful if the source is specific.

steps.title: Ask for the first useful draft
steps.detail: Use InVideo to create the first pass from that source material. Ask for a practical result, not a generic explanation of the topic.

steps.title: Check the draft against the original
steps.detail: Compare names, facts, dates, claims, missing context, and tone against the original input before you edit style or formatting.

steps.title: Change format only after the facts are right
steps.detail: Use Fliki when the result needs a visual, social, presentation, or alternate format. Keep the same source facts so the rewrite does not drift.

steps.title: Polish the final version
steps.detail: Use Kapwing or a manual review pass to tighten wording, remove filler, and make the final result easier to read on a phone or computer.

faqs.question: Can I use AI to handle turning one long video into short vertical clips without losing the core detail?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you choose the tool that matches the input type and then review the output yourself. InVideo is the best starting point, Fliki is the strongest second choice, and Kapwing is the safer fallback when you need a different balance of speed and control.

faqs.question: Which AI tool is better when I need source checking?
faqs.answer: Fliki is usually the better choice when source checking matters, while InVideo is stronger when the job starts with a known set of files or notes.

faqs.question: Is InVideo better than Fliki for this workflow?
faqs.answer: InVideo is better when you want the most direct match for the main input; Fliki is better when the workflow changes enough to justify a different tool.

faqs.question: What should I avoid before I share the result?
faqs.answer: Avoid sharing the first draft until you have checked facts, tone, and any missing context. Use Kapwing as a helper, not a replacement for review.
